One of the best airports I have ever travelled to! Great architecture  excellent securitycheck system  friendly staff.

Going through the airport is a marathon. There is nothing to do in there. There is quite a variety of food places but the walking is literally ridiculous. The toilets are ok but they were closed for a long time while we were waiting. I think they were cleaning and shut the whole place down. It is the less passenger-friendly airport in Europe I know.
The infrastructure is terrible! Not built to the common capacity of passengers... I will not fly here anymore - much more faster will be to fly to Genève and then come to Lyon by train...
Shocking behaviour of security staff - seemed like they were being deliberately slow. Almost missed the flight having arrived 1.5 hours early! Make sure you leave extra time

The new 1B terminal at LYS Airport is very modern with a big shopping center and a very well made architecture. However  the low cost sattelite is a bit dissapointing  but the terminal is really great! Short or even no queues...
A very modern new terminal 1B but still a lot of corridors from the low cost wing. That is a shame...
Brand new terminal which is less convenient to access and way longer waiting times to pass security check.

Lyon Airport Terminal 2 isn't modern at all  and we were on a small terminal with a low ceiling. If you have the choice between this terminal and another  choose the other. A direct tram goes to the centre of Lyon in 30 mins. No wifi

Navette ferroviaire "Rhône Express" pour atteindre l'aéroport hors de prix   ce qui fait que je met qu'une seule étoile. Je n'ai pas encore essayé le nouveau terminal B mais celui des lowcosts n'est pas digne d'un aéroport comme Lyon. Inconfort total  il faut marcher des centaines de mètres ... La seule boutique qu'il y a est très chère.

L'un des aéroports les plus minables d'Europe  3 terminaux éloignés pour le trafic relativement faible  constamment en travaux (on a l'impression d'arriver dans un pays sous développé  avec des cheminements ridicules). On nous fait enregistrer au T1A pour embarquer au T2  les filtres PIF/PAF sont complètement dépassés car l'aéroport est très très étroit et la place manque vite (les plafonds bas n'arrangent rien)  l'hypothèse du rétablissement des contrôles au frontière n'a pas l'air d'avoir été prise en compte lors de la conception (comme c'est le cas actuellement à cause du terrorisme)... Bref quand on voit que certains aéroports reçoivent 2 fois plus de trafic pour la même superficie et sans encombre il faut se poser des questions. Et je ne parle pas de l'accès déplorable avec le raquette du Rhône Express  l'absence de fontaine à eau  des distributeurs qui ne fonctionnent pas  des boutiques hors de prix... la totale !

Peu fonctionnel  étroit et peu de hauteur  commence à devenir vieillot  personnel désagréable ou peu qualifié à quelques exceptions près; St-Exupéry a vraiment perdu en qualité en peu de temps ! Le nouveau terminal lowcost 1B est minable  les terminaux principaux sont vieillots et mal conçus mais corrects. Que dire de la liaison entre les 2 terminaux et le nouveau  sans doute du aux travaux qui semblent s'éterniser : atroce !
Very poor customs arrangement and missing flighton baggage claim monitor casuing delay (had to go to baggage claim desk to figure out where my lugage is)
Fairly small airport. Lots of construction right at the moment (May 2017). Had to transfer from Terminal 1B to Terminal 2. Not a lot of signage to facilitate that. Wifi easy to connect to.

The security process was outrages. It took well over an hour and due to high demand and lack of personel at immigration (only 2 officers were available for hundreds of passengers) the security lanes were temporarily shut down. We had to run to catch the flight which was finally delayed as other fellow passengers were stuck in security as well

Really expensive moving back and forth to the airport. It's kind of unbelievable that rhone express is the only way to reach the airport. It's much cheaper going from the airport to geneva by bus than to lyon by tram...

appalling third rate airport. extremely slow security lines. no where beyond security at F gates to get a drink and the cafe only had 2 very curled up baguettes. got some wine at duty free and the nice assistant went and got us some plastic cups from the staff rest room.

You have to take the bus to get to the rental cars parking  you have to walk for 15 min between the aircraft and the public area in sinister corridors  not heated. FLows are not well managed  as you go back and forth  through the luggage delivery belts area  get in public area and then walk backward  towards the bus stop for rental cars.

Very bad bagage claim .... 45mn waiting (as usual here) and the business/priority were delivered last. Airport crew seemed a bit lazy: while we had to walk down from the plane to the terminal  they were staying nearby the terminal door and waving to us to show the way

Liked : not too big  quick checkin  quick taxi before/after takeoff/landing Didn't like : very poor food service: employers unruly and sometimes nasty + very expensive   bad view over the planes and the runway

Lyon airport lies a good distance outside the city  and as far as I know  the only reasonable ways of getting there are the Rhonexpress train from the center of the city (at a fairly inflated rate of approximately €16 euros for a one way ticket) and taking a taxi (which can set you back €80 or even more depending on the distance and traffic of course). Check-in desks are easy to find  and at least in Lufthansa's case dropping off baggage is usually a breeze  especially if one already has a boarding pass. The security check points  on the other hand  seem to be staffed with people with very limited language skills and no understanding of customer service. All in all  the terminals are pretty basic with no frills and there's a bit of a worn out feeling to the place  especially the restrooms which also tend to be icky. Anyway  this small airport gets the job done for the most part  just don't expect to enjoy the experience to any meaningful degree.
Waiting for luggage takes longer than the ZRH-LYS flight!
Terminal 1 is very boring.

The parking is very well organized. The shorter you stay  the closer to the terminals  and it's all very well indicated. The terminal design is decent  there are a good number of shops for an airport that size. Terminal 3 is crap though (low cost)
miles and miles away from Lyon
A lots of diffrent restaurants and verry good information on signs!
Small  quite confusing but Gare TGV station is in the airport.
Smal airport   TErminal 1-3  LH at T1

-Air France terminal very pleasant -shops and brasseries available -flight informations display very easy to read -route in public zone very fast -boarding zones very comfy (toilets  internet points  viewing areas on taxiways and runways
Terminal 1 is really ugly. Terminal 2 is pretty nice! Try to stay there while you are waiting..
There was a strike at the airport  and it was really difficult to go through security

No permanent check in. Poor gates area with very limited amounts of shops. Business launge outside gates area with poor offer. The only advantage is for Avis preferred rental which is very quick. However you need shuttle bus to go there.
Security scanning was a joke more  like a bun fight. Also a temp building  not very good.
We were parked away  not at an airbridge so it takes a long time by bus to join the terminal. All the airbridges were not used.
The new terminal's construction has began 2 years ago  and now all the Terminal 1B is almost blocked. And the building has delay again !

I arrived late and made my way straight for the bus to the city. These are very regular and the journey doesn't take to long. The main concourse has many food options and information desks and the bus station is a short walk from the terminal exit.

MRS is an easy airport to get to. I was staying near to the train station and from there there is and express bus for 10 euros to the airport. In early morning traffic  the journey took 40 minutes. I was departing from T1 Hall B. There was a lot of construction work going on and the route there was convoluted and not well signposted. Security took about 5 minutes. Once through there are few facilities. I couldn't see a hydration station. That was a pity  as I'd emptied my water bottle in the main terminal before I realised that I had a long walk to Hall B. I was not in the departure lounge for long  so the lack of facilities was not a problem.
Very good airport but the check in of Ryanair it’s a little bit complicated and he not work all the time.

Only bad part about exiting is these doors that control the flow of people i think and they don’t shut smoothly they slam on you very hard but managed to get through without being hit just some people got stuck

Horrible airport  not even sure what positive aspect can even be mentioned. Chaotic  old  overcrowded  unfriendly staff in all areas  unorganized boarding  tiny and overcrowded lounge without windows and way to less bathrooms (that are dirty on top). In already was my 3rd time there  absolutely not recommendable.
Aéroport équivalant à un aérodrome  indigne de la 2 eme ville de France: site à raser et à reconstruire.
Aéroport en travaux  trafic convenable  le futur est prometteur et les travaux qui s’y font ne seront que bénéfiques
French border police sat there looking at the growing queue for a long time before letting anyone through. No sense of efficiency at all
不知道行李人員是罷工還是怎樣的，等了一小時才出行李，全機乘客都等到發火

I used Terminal 2 (MP2) for my Ryanair flight  its a very basic terminal with a restaurant  a shop selling souvenirs and food and drink and an overpriced duty free shop - it is cheaper to buy a litre of whiskey in my local shop than it was in duty free  even the same brand.

There's a shuttle service from the Saint Charles railway station; the bus stop at the airport is about 100-200m from the termina entrance. Check-in is pretty easy to find  yet getting to the gate area was somewhat difficult  maybe because the passport control had changed how to go there. I didn't have much time to look for shopping possibilities but there was a duty free shop close to my gate. There is free WiFi at the airport.

Despite the terminal buildings appear to be old the airport is pleasant with a great view to the runways thanks to big windows in the boarding areas. Check in are most of the time fluent and the airport is clean. The main fault to me is the constant music in the boarding areas that becomes quickly annoying ! (This shall apply for Hall 1  3 & 4; MP2 is the exact opposite).

Good airport facilities  to get to the security check you must get through the check-in desks... A strange way to access... Anyway  no restrooms into the area once you're checked-in... Seats are not very confortable...

The airport must facilitate the access to Marseille with train. I wait too long for the shuttle between terminals and Vitrolles AMP station. The bagage delivery was also very slow... 40 minutes ! So  this is a sympathic airport but too "regional-styled" for the 2nd biggest city of France !
Welll  Marseille Provence Airport... i don't have really much to say. it's just an airport how an airport needs to be !! everything is super.

EasyJet is in Terminal 2 (mp2)  the low-cost terminal. Dreadful atmosphere. The worst terminal I've ever flown from. :( Even EasyJet gate attendants don't know how to handle a mobile boarding pass. They "had" to write me a useless cardboard BP. It could have been worse  though. We could have been flying with RyanAir.
very nice and beautifull
Obsolete terminal 1  not enought seat to have a rest  check-in area not adapt for a lot of people - Inside bording building  seat are very hard like cement - very disappointed
A quick easy to use low cost airport.

Very nice approach at MRS ! Fully manual. I was in the cockpit during all flight along.
Super

Aéroport vraiment trop peu fréquenté  loin de tout  pas pratique pour les accès si on n'a pas sa voiture sur place. Pourquoi ne choisit-on pas plutôt de développer des vols vers d'autres villes françaises au départ de Luxembourg ? A quoi sert le fait d'avoir un aéroport déficitaire en Lorraine ?

This airport is at least for strangers difficult to find  because of bad indicated directions and few street signs. The airport itself is not interesting  due to very limited flights. The destination and frequency network is inconvenient. In general  there are just few domestic destinations in France served. The infrastructure looks outdated and boring in many ways. The ticket prices for Hop by Air France  are by far to expensive. Jetairfly offers only some flights  for local Moroccan people living in Lorraine/Moselle to Morocco. Watch out: These are no tourist or holiday destinations. This is also the case for some seasonal flights to Algeria by air Algerie. Finally  you must be extremely flexible to find a flight from the middle of nowhere to almost nowhere. The location is far away from everything and everywhere. The region maybe needs the airport  but many passengers simply don´t need this poor airport.
To bad there are so few flights and it is poorly connected. Wanting to be close to Metz AND Nancy  it ended up in the middle of nowhere.
Aéroport indispensable au développement économique de la région Lorraine  accessible et de taille humaine : à privilégier pour vos déplacements en métropole.

Perfect small airport. The 5 stars are relative to the size. As a small airport  it is easily accessible but only by car/taxi. Check-in (not used this time) is easy and friendly. Security is friendly and there are hardly ever long waiting lines. Facilities are ok (bar  sandwiches  tax-free shop with quite a bit of choice) but not comparable to large airports. The lounge is nice  but also not comparable to large airports  both in comfort and in choice of food and beverages.
Really nice and modern airport. There is a shuttle bus (every 30 minutes) to the airport. There aren't many flights so it's not a busy airport. You'll get really quickly through security.
Bus from airport only once an hour? C'mon you french lazy suckers! Otherwise cozy and modern airport. Adding more toilets would be a great idea thou.

le terrain de jeu est une super idée mais le contrôle de sécurité qui tourne à la fouille complète de toute famille y compris le gosse de 3 ans parce que leur alarme à l'explosif détecte les lessives ... oui je lave mes sac des fois  apparemment c'est un crime

Really nice airport: free WiFi  fast check-in. The air terminal is large and bright  just like the departure lounge. All the airport is very clean  silent and well organied. There are many duty free shops. The access to the airport is really easy. Parkings are not very expensive.

Good airport. But the public Wi-Fi is free for only 10 mins... It's easy to get to airport  the shuttle can be take at "Place de l'Europe" in Montpellier wich is fine and accessible in tramway. The check-in is fast and easy  same for security check.

The airport overall is not bad  it's easy to reach it by public transportation  whilst by taxi is a little bit expensive. They have a really nice open lounge for smockers  not great food and retail service but more than acceptable. Actually  the prices inside the airport are not even that high as usual. The big problem  which really affects the overall grade  is that the free WiFi is for only 10 minutes! yes  10 minutes  freaking unbelievable...but it just traces the french society. They are French and not German  nice people but still they tend to drown in an inch of water...it is not acceptable that an international airpot just gives you 10 minutes to surf the net. Just a little comparison: in Munich you have 24 hours...
Small and quite. A good airport for both small and large airlines.
J'ai adoré la navette aeroport qui t'ammene directement depuis centre ville jusqu'à l'aéroport. Même si l'aéroport est petit  les services offertes sont assez pour tous

Small airport with even smaller mentality. Not equipped for the current volumes and whilst easy to navigate  you can be caught in slow queues going through security. Airport needs more than just an upgrade!

One of the worst airport in Europe. Facilities are old  always packed with people  no sitting space  it's always hot  no way to entertain kids  the food offer is very poor  security check pretty intense.
Too busy  not built to accommodate premium travelers and dated facilities including bathrooms that would probably not even be tolerated in prison!!!
No lounge at the airport! Also  not that many options for food and you can't go through security before knowing the gate  which is displayed about 2h before the flight.

Bagage perdu à Nantes à l'aller : ma valise n'a même pas quitté l'aéroport. Au retour  attente de 20 minutes avant l'apparition des premiers bagages sur le tapis. Toujours très long à cet aéroport. Service bagages à revoir. Des aéroports plus grands avec plus de trafic ont moins de mal avec les valises. Salles d'attente pas assez grandes
Really small airport. Lounges are accessed before security. If you are flying in a smaller aircraft you will be boarded by bus  then you need to use the old hall which is poorly serviced

Petit aéroport  mal conçu. La livraison des bagages peut s'avérer long même si le nombre des passagers n'est pas important. Agents de la sécurité et de l'immigration impolis… Toilettes non nettoyées. Aucun personnel pour vous guider (ou alors vous risquez de tomber sur un agent désagréable…)! Vivement le réaménagement de cet aéroport !

Aéroport petit et peu fonctionnel. Les gens patientent et font la queue au milieu du hall. Aucun personnel n'est présent pour renseigner les voyageurs. Toilettes non nettoyées  voir "dégueulasses". Personnel de la sécurité sympathique mais agent de l'immigration froid et irrespectueux.
The automatic check in is really convinient and there was no line at security control. I arrived 40min prior to my boarding(one hour before flight) and i had time to eat and do shopping.
Horrible service  all shops and restaurants were closed at 21:30 which is horrble  also the baggage drops was delayed by an HOUR because of the french
Passport control was fast. The airport terminal was clean and modern. Baggage claim did take a little bit of time though.
The passport control process was smooth and fast. Small terminal  but clean and modern. Baggage claim did take a bit of time.

This airport is the worst in Europe. Even though security was smooth  the boarding experience was the worst I have seen on any flight. Four flights  including ours were boarding from two gates in the small rest-stop like area. There were no buses  which we had to wait for 30min to get after which roughly 150 people were crammed onto. Staff were unhelpful  unfriendly and any concerns will not be handled very well.

Great airport and awesome possibilities to enjoy Nice by tram and bus! Tickets are cheap and tram and bus bring you all along the coast to see as much as you want! Many restaurants directly at the beach  little bit expensive but that's luxurious Nice!
Super schnell aus dem Flieger zum Hotel ca. 15 min nach Landung im Hotel gewesen (Ibis stylen) gut zu laufen
Security moaned that I hadn't removed my camera from my bag  at busy times not much rom to sit. Lounge is reasonable but no good views across the airport.
Very quick processing... little queues. With your boarding pass ready  from the airport entrance to your flight's gate at Terminal 1 just takes you about 10 min!

First time to the NCE airport  my experience was very good to this airport. Getting to the airport is very easy by public transportations with the city line tram L2 witch you can take it from the terminal 1 or 2  there is also buses which you can use the same ticket for both and it’s cheap. I didn’t had a check in baggage but the check in area was quiet and there was a lot of check-in desks for each airline which is easy to find your check in desk. The security check was fast with a lot of security check desks and also the staff was fast and efficient. The terminal facilities for a small airport like this one are great  you have a specialized area for food services  there is also a lot of retail stores after passing through the security check and you can find what you need easy and fast.

First time to the NCE airport  my experience was very good to this airport. Getting to the airport is very easy by public transportations with the city line tram L2 witch you can take it from the terminal 1 or 2  there is also buses which you can use the same ticket for both and it’s cheap. The terminal facilities for a small airport like this one are great  you have a specialized area for food services  there is also a lot of retail stores after passing through the security check. The emigration/customs are fast and efficient. Also if you have a connection flight you can accès very fast to the boarding area. About the bagage claim you can arrive there fast and easy and you can receive your baggage quite fast.

Ne pas arriver avant 2h du vol  sous peine de se faire renvoyer désagréablement par l'agent à l'entrée de l'enregistrement des bagages. Même si vous etes en fauteuil roulant av3c 7n assistant de l'aéroport
Poor quality of Terminal 1. Lazy and unfriendly staff. Only positive is perfect connection to the Nice city centre by tram.
The airport didn't have enough staff to push the staircase towards the airplane so we could get out upon landing. We had to sit and wait for ages.
More than an hour wait before luggage was on carpet  it was unloaded but not put on the carpet. Only after going to complain something was done about it.

Decent airport but crap when it comes to food alergens. I’m dairy intolarent and all there was to eat at 19:30 was sandwiches - all options had cheese. Only 1 didn’t but had tuna - im allergic to that :/
A very crowded airport. Staff are friendly and generally not too long lines for check in. Terminal 2 though is quite ugly.
A French airport. Nothing special about it. The accessibility is fine. My flight departed on time but I don't know if that's the case foor a lot of flights.

Extremely slow border passage. Not adapted to the modern way of traveling when borders have to control covid documents. It took me more than an hour with only a half full plane. Don’t want to arrive with a bigger one!
The average french airport. Everything was quick and very easy. The bagage claim area wasn't to busy but also not empty. Overall the airport was fine.

Check in was absolutely terrible. Extremely unorganised mess; -not enough desks open -at least 50% skipping the line one way or another -nobody at the airport taking responsibility for the chaos taking place.
To get out of the airport  I did not have too much trouble  the airport is well described  I have no other remarks to make.

Terminal 2 is the newest. Facilities are good. Access from A8 highway convenient. P6 long term parking is a good option when booked online. Fast Track for security was not available in July due to COVID-19. Infinity lounge is located upstairs  entrance near the junction between the round building and the square building.

The airport was very clean and nice. Getting to the airport was comfy  even trams get there. Terminal was pleasant  very good. Wi-Fi has been a problem. Couldn't connect to it most of the time. Food was good  ate a sandwich there. Lounge was very comfy and clean. Customs were handled very quickly. The baggage claim was a bit of a problem. After all 5/5
Terminal 2  B gates lounge is quiet but quite small and poor in terms of food offering despite the fact we were there lunch time...

My handluggage was taken to the hold because of lack of space on board. They didn't load the luggage and four consecutive flights left Nice without my luggage. Only on the fifth flight my luggage was loaded  55 hours after my arrival in Nice my luggage was delivered at my address. Pretty scandalous.

T2: For Kiss & Fly/Depose minute ask to be dropped by a crossing to T2. T2 is AF & Skyteam but also Easyjet & Ryanair. Just be aware the terminal is quite large. Both check-in and gates are spread out.

Departing from terminal 2. I went to the airport by Uber. Unfortunately public transportation are quite rubbish. They will improve when the tramway will start to operate but it will be handy only if you stay in Nice. If you plan to stay elsewhere around (Cannes  Antibes  Monaco...) you need to take a car or Uber or cab. Terminal isn't too big so you can quickly go from landside to airside (security and immigration are usually quite quick). Not a lot of services available but still good for a small terminal.
Arrival at the airport terminal 2. You are quite quickly outside not a lot of walking to do. Immigration control is usually quite quick as well even if you have only two agents operating.

Nice Airport offers great views of the French Riviera during landing and take off. Terminal 2 is convenient. Access by car from highway A8  national road N7 (RD6007) and Promenade des Anglais boulevard. Parking P5 is closest and more expensive (though special rates are available when booking online in advance). Parkings P6  P7 can be booked online for more than 3 days  and parking P9 for longer periods. Public transport: tramway and bus. Note that bus 250 going to Villeneuve Loubet  Antibes  Golfe Juan and Vallauris only runs from 08:10 to 20:20 (leaving airport) and 06:30 to 19:10 (leaving Vallauris). The choice of restaurants and shops is quite good (no Asian food though). Security check is efficient and quick compared to other airports.
Clean and spacious airport. Only got food in terminal two is provided by Jamie Oliver which is no good if you have morals.
Do not spend any more time on the airport. Crowded  amost nowhere to sit down and everything closes at 10PM
Cant handle the 350. Long wait for the bus! Poor service from handling company Aviapartners. Airport facility not up to its routes network. Need upgrades
T2 need to be upgraded. Managed by a company who is known to squeeze the last cent without caring about safety
Le wifi ne marche pas  pour les bagages  le tapis qui ne fonctionne pas... Sa ne donne pas envie d'y aller
Le wifi ne marchait pas  sur l'autoroute  très mal indiqué  une attente très longue pour passer les contrôles de sécurité avec l'A380 juste à côté...
Uber taxi much cheaper than standard taxi. Tram from city to Terminal is under construction and will be very useful.
Taxis are an issue if there are more than 4 passengers and it resulted in having to call for a large vehicle and cost much more.
Great airport  lot of plane  cross dutyfree mandatory (t2 B)  security check employee not very polite paf busy at arrival (-> waiting)  closed on departure (-> waiting) anybody can take your luggage

Flight got delayed (2 hrs). No information  screen was showing "go to gate" (we had to use the Vueling app to see the new schedule). All shops were closed and the only drink dispensers available were not working.
They want to see the boarding pass 4 times and when departing from T1 Sector B  it will be small and almost with no facilities and no lounge.

Easily accessible by public transport from any part of Cote D'Azur  good infrastructure  nice people. Navigation is good  too. Security check may take a while  each time of my 5 times there I had to wait in a line.

Terminal 1 departure experience. A little surprised check in for flight opens 2 hours 30 minutes instead of usual 3 hours..... Had to re-register WiFi every hour to continue using free WiFi  little inconvenience for me  to be honest. No window at departure airside lounge as I like to look at ramp activities and take off/landing on the runway......oh well.

Experience at Terminal 1 which is older than T2! Long queue at passport control with two desks opened and two busy flights arrived - took a while to finally get through to baggage reclaim from passport control. Staff at passport control just opened my passport  looked at it for one microsecond and handed me back  interesting experience(!) Had to re-register WiFi every hour to continue using free WiFi  little inconvenience for me  to be honest. Whether or not Orly is easy to reach depends very much from where you are in Paris. If you're close to one of the RER lines B or C  it is okay (though neither of the lines goes to Orly Airport directly  while Charles de Gaulle has a direct RER connection). In most cases  you will have to change once or twice to get to or from Orly  which is a mess if you have luggage. (Especially if it involves a metro trip  with the turntiles in your way  a lot of stairs going up and down etc.) The airport itself felt crowded and stressful to me. The terminal building (South) does not follow any clear architectural line so that orientation is not always easy even though the building isn't huge. Often  fundamental walking paths like entrances to the building or the ways proceeding to security check seem like some sort of hidden secondary tracks where you are not really supposed to be as a passenger. All parts of the airport chronically lack seats. If ever you manage to get one of the few seats  it will still be noisy  crowded and hectic all around you. The check-in process (for Air Berlin  handled by the local contractor) was painfully slow. The counters opened no second earlier than 2hrs before departure. No separate counter for bagage drop-off was available  so even though I was checked in  I had to wait in line. It took the person at the counter 35 minutes to check in the 8 passengers waiting before me. The lines at the other counter didn't go any faster.

Middle of nowhere!

had to wait 1+ hours for customs... they have a bunch of booths but only like 3 people working??? each booth had a literal mile long line waiting to get processsed. they literally started passing out water too... plan for a few extra hours (depends on time of day you're coming in/out)
Terminal 3 good for charter flights. Quick and efficient check in and security. A sufficient number of shops and very good WIFI.

Only for terminal 3. Terminal easily accessible  immigration time is good and rather quick. Baggage recovered quickly and in good condition. And presence of certain services. And WIFI very good. Good for a charter-only terminal.
Stop over in CDG after a flight from MRS before. Too bad we can't go from a terminal to another  I had to stay in the T 2F for 4 hours
I personnaly think CDG is a good airport but there is so many people waiting its hard to know where is your gate because there are so many.
Nice airport  unfortunately underrated by most bur better than most. Specially other ones like Frankfurt or Arlanda.

Se questo è l'aeroporto delle olimpiadi sono messi male. Check in lenti e non ci sono i desk per il solo drop-off. Ai controlli di sicurezza devi ancora tirare fuori tutto dovendo utilizzare minimo 3 ceste. Pochissimi schermi enuncianti i gate. Un disabile è dovuto recarsi all'aeromobile senza accompagno. E 45' per un imbarco bloccando tutto per fare 4 etichette per bagagli da mandare in stiva. Se tornerò a Parigi sarà nel 2025

So big. You can walk for hours. Luxury. Everything is so expensive. I got lost many times. Anticipate by arriving early. Far from Paris and expensive trip. Hotels are expensive and far. Expensive also to reach hotels and shuttles don't work all the day! You will maybe have to take a taxi.

Following extensive renovations the airport is modern and easy to navigate  there are large touch screen navigation passenger portals where you can enter your flight details and you will be shown the route to your gate or anything else you may want to find

The airport seems to have a poor layout. We landed in fog  so I couldn't see anything  but we landed on one of the far runways and our taxy required us to hold to cross another active runway. There are regular trains from the airport to Gard du Nord.
Busy and crowded. Security  despite sky priority  was chaotic and busy. Took about an hour to transfer from Schengen to non Schengen
Very rude security guards with extremely limited choices of restaurants after passing the security check.
Big airport needs lots of time for transfers. Recommend 2 hours and that's if you are staying in the same terminal. (Terminal 2)

Getting to CDG is easy with regular trains from the city. However  it's a long walk from the train station to Terminal 2B. This is my first time departing from CDG in many years and I would think again before departing from T2B again. The security queue stretched for about 12 full lengths of the hall and took an hour for me to clear. This included my bag being pulled for a random bag check. I'd been notified earlier in the day that my flight would be delayed  but this was not reflected on the departure board; resulting in many people (myself included) rushing through immigration to the gate. This was unfortunate as there are few food and drink options. on the other side. The lounge has plenty of seating including lounger chairs  though.
No jet bridge as usual and very poor arrival experience. Passport control outages  cramped arrival area with dense crowd … a god way to start a new pandemic…
Flight took from the Terminal 3  which seems to be a more peaceful terminal than the Terminal 2. No too much people and only one security check. Not much food facilities  though.
It was a good airport you know. And was very cool but the way to get out was a nightmare it took me 30mins to get out to the taxi rank.

Amazing Airport  security is worse than TSA though. The Star Alliance lounge had a great buffet  and was comfortable for a long wait before my flight. Wi-Fi worked well  the facilities and entertainment were great  especially if one were to have a long layover. Metro to the airport was very crowded. I wouldn’t recommend it while a world rugby championship was going on.
at my time of travel  CDG airport was very crowded  lines at security  check-in and immigration were very long.
Train fron terninal 2M to terminal 2K was not working. We had to walk ( with hand luggage) in the underground of terminal 2.

The terminals are really poor on facilities  no water fountains  and once you are at your gate you cant get back to the other facilities because you need to go through passport control and airport security allowed someone with 4 bottles of water through security and was really confusing with the instructions  I was told to put 1 bag in 1 tray only and then I was told by a different staff member that I should just put them all in one tray. Its really confusing but was quick!
A lot of improvements from when I was there in 2022. Better organisation and better attitude from staff. Only issues was the number of gate changes for my flight.

This airport is one of the most unpractical airport ever  signage is not easy to follow and if you follow everything thoroughly you might still end up in the wrong terminal. It's clean  food offer and shopping is good but when you have to move from one terminal to another it becomes a nightmare  and staff is not very helpful. On top of this  security checks are always packed with people and staff impolite and rude.

Amazing transfer in CDG. Modern and clean terminal  there was a HUGE variety of shops and restaurants  and the little train that takes you from terminal to terminal is very fun to ride. Signs were in French  English  and in Mandarin.
The accès to the airport was good but when we went to the check in  the staff was very rude and the self check in didn't work  we almost missed our flight due to the staff.
Smooth transfer at CDG. Nice terminal  and the signage makes everything that much easier. The selection of shops is nice  and T2E is clean and modern.
Good airport for its size  immigration is rubbish though. Security isn't bad and once through at least in 2E it's clean enough including toilets. Plenty of eating choices and duty free.
shitty boarding procedure delays everything  no real place to eat at any of F terminal  way to small for this operations … just does not give AF a chance to operate efficiently at all
Sorry  but it’s the worst Airport I have bin to. The drop-Bag  Lounge  Security Check and everything was bad.
A bit small and outdated for the star Alia ce Lou he   didn’t have time to visit the new terminal but it’s very nice to see it refurbished
I have heard that Charles de Gaulle is a terrible airport to be transit in  however didn't feel that in this case when it was only a point to point flight.
When arriving from an airport located in the Schengen zone and connecting to a flight to Montréal  at least you don't have to go through security in that direction (as opposed to the opposite).
The crazy thing when landing at CDG  when you have to take a connecting flight  is that you have to go through security again. It's really stupid.
And we have again winner for one of the worse airports in Europe. Chaos  arrogant security check staff

I really do not get why there are automatic terminals for US passports only... Otherwise  the lounges are quite good and there are USB (A and C) + Schuko (220 V) plugs for charging with every seat  which is great if they all are working (check it in advance). Security check was quick. There are some good shops inside. Boarding may be organized better.

A busy and large airport. Getting between terminals is quite easy with CDGVal train. Coffees and restaurants are quite small however and you need to queue. Terminals are well labeled and there are signs for trains  taxis and buses  so just follow them.

11 years ago  when we went  there were lang check-in lines and the pressure on the personel was pretty high. There were a number of reasonably anger people who  like us  had been stoon in the line for what seemed like ages. Thankfully our wait ulitimately came to an end and we were allowed through. Hopefully it is better now as I will be flying there next month - with Austral!
Exercise Caution! Transferring between two international flights  we went through no less than four security lines  which took over an hour. (Thankfully  we still made our next flight - barely.)
Upper floor food court was closed for refurbishment. So no hot food. Only baps and sandwiches available. Very disappointing

Check'IN was an impertinence. There are constant announcements that the security check takes a long time but nevertheless the check'in (all employees were there) was denied. After that  of course  the chaos arose because the employees on site rather played with the mobile phone than to work. Lounge there was no suitable  food was overpriced because you had no other option.

The airport (Terminal 1) is far too small for the masses of passengers that are handled here. The baggage claim did not work at all  there were suitcases everywhere  the display was suddenly on finished although only 3 suitcases have come. The Air India next to us has received the suitcases only over an hour after arrival.

Cet aéroport est très bien mais il y a quelque petit problème les portillons qui sonne pour rien le temps d'attente ce n'est pas la première fois que j'y vais mais toujours les mêmes problèmes du coup 4 étoiles

amazing airport  super clean and incredibly bright and updated. easy to get to most things (except the bathrooms) in terminal 1  also had a playstation 5 demo that you could play with while waiting for your flight. 10/10 airport
Awesome experience travelling between terminal 1 and terminal 2. Train was quick and easy. Immigration were quick/efficient and even smiled!
The security check was a bit disorganized going from the arrivals to departures. There wasn't much clearly signed that said what you should take out.
2E lounge is outdated Food for breakfast staying to long does not look to be fresh anymore ( cheeses were dry and some harden )
Termianl 3 has only 1 food option afeter security. Getting to the airport is very expensive. Secuirity was fast.
This airport was designed as a joke  no? I can't see any other reason behind its complete mess of an experience.
Beautiful airport with lots of shops  natural light and places to sit. No place to refill a water bottle.
All shops and food areas are closed  and everything is built terribly. This airport was made for looks and not efficiency
Huge airport but a little complicated to use it... Immigration and security check could be better organized and faster.
30-minute queue for security check for the priority path. Lounge bounded  difficult to find a place
Gate change not announced  announcements overall very unclear. Check in for some flights about 10 min walk from RER station.

The airport was very crowded but the design of the airport is terrible. Boarding and deboarding requires travellers to go through a door that intersects with each other  other than that everything else is average.

nothing is done for customers in CDG. I had to catch a connection and the transfer from one side of terminal 2 to the other side of the terminal is not efficient at all. And very difficult to have a smile from the people working in the airport.
Terminal 2B/2D is clean and very clear But the new lounge Exim was noisy and people disrespectful to the others pax

Still my least favourite airport to connect to. When changing terminals  ensure you have AT LEAST 45 minutes. I timed myself today and needed 37 on a fairly smooth operation. It’ll be tight if your flight gets delayed.

Horrible Airport. Transfers take hours  long bus rides  extremely long walks  extremely long lines at passport checks (3 auto-gates for all EU passport holders meant we had to wait 60+ minutes  the non-EU passport line was even worse).

We had some trouble in finding the public service issuing the NAVIGO card. Th eoffice was very busy with a long line. The train from the airport to the metro has too many stops so it takes a long time to reach the hotel.
needed to take 2 separate shuttle buses to transfer to a different terminal  so didn't manage to catch the 2nd flight on time. Too big of an airport  too crowded  everything is slow
Public transports in Paris and attitude of control. Be avare of them- they just hunting on tourists.

o Aeroporto de CDG acaba sendo um pouco complicado devido aos 50km distantes do centro de Paris. Mesmo através da conexão via trem acaba se tornando demorado mas é o principal aeroporto da França e o que nos permite "chegar mais longe". Última vez que passamos por lá o aeroporto apresentava necessidade de melhorias.

Operational challenges and maintenance-linked cancellations meant there were few options to get to CDG other than with a car  making the whole thing much more expensive. Check-in at T3 was handled swiftly by the Alyzia staff servicing the Volotea flight; the queue at security was short and the check  efficient and polite. This is a basic terminal handling mostly low-cost travel  and facilities are positioned accordingly - a few shops  food options from the fridges & shelves of general retailers  OK WiFi  no lounge available.

Literally every staff member that should coordinate people sent us to wrong place. During the stay we asked 5 staff members in total about our check-in  gate  lounge and terminal position and all of them were not just useless  but they made us go the wrong way to spend more time seeking. They literally decreased value  the airport is wasting money on such staff members. That's the first airport out of about 80 I visited that I report to. Wonderland experience of such incompetence :D

I brought my cello with me and it was well handled  everything worked out although I would say the security check was... rushed  to say the least. My last problem was with the hygiene  but the airport is so big I can't really blame it. Apart from those things  nice airport. Big. Some good food places too.
shitty food selection in 2F  pier always too crowded  no-sense tow-in design for all incoming aircrafts killing 10min on a good day ...
Very high traffic to the airport. Too long waiting for everything (checkin  security  baggage  queue in dute free)
Very high traffic to/from aiport  bad navigation at the airport  too long waiting for everything (checkin  security  baggage)  small dute free area  too much reconstructions at the airport terminals.
My main gripe with the airport wan the requirement to give personal info for access to the WiFi. Other than that  a very pleasant experience.
CDG is good by modern standards  only immigration was a pain. WiFi works  really good transport facilities plenty to eat and the lounge is spacious.

Disappointed by the personal in the security check without politeness. They were aggressive   they asked me to take out all my lenses of my camera to put it on different trays. That’s crazy … ! I’ve traveled in Russia/ USA / all around the world   any personal of an airport asked me to do that ..

Charles de Gaulle was quite nice  the architecture of T2E was really nice  the only bad thing was that security had stopped my bag and put it aside just because I has goat cheese  it took them 30 minutes before they actually started checking the bag  and by the time they were done we'd have to run to the gate and were the last passengers  we'd boarded right before they were about to close the gate.

Charles de Gaulle wasn't the best  it took 1 and a half hours only to get out the airport  you had to walk for 15 minutes  then take a train to the other terminal  then walk for another 10 minutes before you'd even get to passport control and then walk another 20 minutes  it was quite frustrating  RoissyBus apparently said it operates every 15 minutes and I'd waited 25 minutes for the bus  so overall not the best experience.
Arriving by RER is cheap  however  TGV prices are very expensive. Check in took 50 minutes with no organisation. Security staff were very rude and shouted at passengers for no reason.
Very cool airport. I haven't slept in 24 hours when I was there so it was probably my tiredness high speaking when I say the airport architecture was really cool.

Extremely slow controls and inefficient system of baggage claim. RER B inside the airport does not work on weekend so passengers are crammed inside tiny buses without any respect of COVID rules. The airport should reconsider public transport from and to Paris during the weekends.
Only one working ATM in terminal Only one security line open  took quite a while to get through. No food shops open.
No clear signage. Had an appointment for a covid test and couldn’t find where it was. Only found it when we went down to get the train.

Security check was rude. Renovations got me walking a lot. Gates are super far from check in  si expect a very long walk. Immigration line was very slow. Air France Lounge was really great  totally worth it  but do arrive to the airport at least 3 hours prior to your international departure.
CDG is always difficult to get to  although the traffic was ok this time. Took a taxi instead of RER since that was really slow due to construction.

The new CDG 2B/D terminal has just been refurbished and looks great But as usual  Paris Aéroport offers only premium services and shop where everything is obviously expensive even when flying with low cost !

Poorly security check as it takes over an hour to complete. The reasons for this delayed process are 1) bad queuing system and 2) ground staffs are not connected to each other and poorly communicable in English.
Charles de Gaulle airport is pretty good  in general good hygiene  the terminal facilities are a bit mazes though and also the check-in moved without us knowing but it could be in any airport so… :)
Big airport with lounges and distractions. We could see airplanes like the a380 or the b777 doing their take-off. Plus for the bagages  that was fast as the embarquement was.
AF lounge discontinued its service for Skyteam elite plus members. Which make a whole idea of aria alliances a joke.
COVID protocol sucks  no social distance same gate for two flights in less than 30 minutes  and the rest were empty

Business lounge at 2F closes at 18:00. Restaurants and cafés are closed except for take away  so everyone’s eating on the seats around the terminal. The smoking booth was open  with everyone crammed inside taking off their masks to enjoy the smoke  perfect combination for a Covid cluster.
Easy boarding off the plane. As french citizen  PARAFE (automated border crossing) is very convenient. Maybe the aiport needs a refresh  feels very old.
This airport is disgusting. I really don’t like it. They come up to you and randomly search you for no reason. The airport smells like smoke and it is just disgusting.

The airport is incredible. Very new. The only thing that I didn’t like  was the “check-in” of AirFrance. The system was down and I arrived late to the gates. If you don’t want problems  arrive even earlier. Get to the airport as early as possible.

CDG is a very easy airport to get around though like other French airports the pax flows are not always logical. Terminal 2A check-in is 1st floor  security and immigration ground floor and departure gates 1st floor. Otherwise  clean with lots of shops and restaurants.
Overall  the experience was okay. The review wasn't as great since I never had the time to in-depth look through these things since I had no time  but overall the service was pretty decent!

This airport is efficient  but they had long questions for passport control and wifi wasn't to good. There were a few shops open though and there was a marks and Spencer to eat in. We also got our bags rather quickly.
2F is overcrowded  lacking seats and rest areas. The lounge is crowded as well. But a new lounge is under construction.
like i said it’s confusing but once getting there at the terminal you’re supposed to be it’s getting easy to find things and a lot of nice views of the runway
Terminal 2G is far away from the other terminals. So long ways are necessary if you have a Transfer. Also the facilities should be better and the waiting area could be cleaner.
Terminal 3 is maybe not the best terminal in the world but the access is easy and the procedures are fast and easy going.
Huge  transport between terminals is a bus. A BUS! The country that invented the TGV  uses a bus between terminals. Barely made my flight.
No connection between terminals 2D and 2F without leaving the air space  which means another security check  so I was forced to throw what I had just bought from the duty free on my first flight :'(
Used priority check-in thanks to business class  designated check-in area leading to fast lane immigration and security  not enough retail in terms of quick to grab fast food (very French...)
All in French. When asked to find an english leaflet or info : ''sorry  this is France''. very impolite.

Far walk from gate to baggage claim  can’t complain too much against 60s design. Baggage came out pretty quickly. The cab was right outside which was good  the road was congested and took time back home.
EEW. DISGUSTING. SO GROSS. The terminal was a DISK SHAPED CONCRETE STRUCTURE. Everyone smoked and smelled. Baggage took FOREVER

Was here recently for a tranfer flight AMS-CDG-MLA. ATC Strike - 40 minutes delay inbound. Lost my bag (even with 1:15 hour layover time) Make you walk 30 minutes and go through security again - even though you travel completely in the Schengen area. Priority lines are half the speed of normal lines. Personnel simply doesn't care about travel classes. Avoid this horrible airport at all cost.
Same terminal transfers are a breeze. This side of 2F (gate 59) was a lot more relaxed with the restaurant tunnel than the other side
Drop-off des valises super long  4 guichets ouverts pour check-in  1 seul pour le drop-off Des personnes sans check-in qui vont dans la queue drop-off également

This is a review of departure from terminal 3. Access to terminal 3 isn't easy. The closest you can get is to Roissypole if you come by tube (RER) from Paris  which is 10 minutes walking distance from terminal 3. In my case it was worse since I came be coach due to strikes and it dropped me terminal 1. The journey from terminal 1 to terminal 3 took me 15 minutes running all over the place. So better assume 20 minutes. Security departure wasn't clearly marked inside the terminal despite being it a very small one. Security took me 15 minutes to finish. Police border checks was very quick since I was the only one. On the airside part there isn't much to do. Usual boutiques and I think a couple of food places. Boarding is always done by bus in terminal 3 which is a little bit painful.
Terminal 1  good access  nice retro feeling  not much choice in terms of fast food  security check at each finger very bad design (though good for aircraft turnarounds).
Love the airport. For architecture lovers it is a delight of a design. The pedestrian flow is excellent  and if you seem lost there are many employees that can be of assistance.
Departing from terminal 2E. Security check was a bit long. Food providers not as numerous as one would expect from the main terminal of Air France. Terminal is very quiet.

Departing terminal 2D. Terminal is small so moving inside of it is quick. Security is quite efficient and immigration process is very quick. You don't have a lot of food choices especially once you have crossed immigration (which is way after you crossed security). Toilets are clean. Terminal isn't noisy. Reaching the airport is somehow easy but disappointing for a big city like Paris. The best option is to take RER B from Châtelet or Gare du Nord. If you're lucky you will have the one that doesn't stop at all between Gare du Nord and the airport. Otherwise you'll stop in some of the suburban towns North of Paris which will increase your journey's time.

Arrived to terminal 3. I used to hate this terminal mainly due to lengthy immigration process at arrival. But now they put automatic gates which make this part much more smooth. The main drawback of this terminal is that public transportation aren't close and you need to walk almost 10 minutes to reach Central station close to terminal 1. Walking is done outside which  in winter  can be quite cold.

Arrival to terminal 2D. Exiting the terminal is quite quick since it's a very small one. Crossing immigration took some time since no automatic gates and only two agents. No distinction between European and non European passports. Due to public transportation strike in Paris I had to take the private bus company called "le bus direct" which is quite expensive. Need to change terminal and need to wait quite long before bus arrived.

Pas de coupe file ou mal contrôle plus de monde qu en économique.. Personnel nombreux mais ne contrôle pas les.passzged.priority Lounge air France une catastrophe petit deux WC pas de.place.assise dans une cave ....
Pas de skypriority supervisé tout le monde prend la file Pas.de.skypriority pour contrôle douane

I remember the flight attendants for American Airlines were very engaging especially since they had never seen a passport like mine before.They asked me about the symbolism of the coat of arms which featured an image of two men.For them this was quite unusual.
Nice modern building with excellent facilities but staff are second rate overall and are very slow to deal with customers
Starting to look tired  but the main criticism is the sheer lack of food outlets in Terminal 2K. The satellite terminal 2G is really basic and looks unfinished

Confusing as always. Annoying that you have to clear security again  even if transferring. I try to avoid CDG when possible. Especially the signage and way finding are terrible. Automatic passport gates are usually out of order. Staff is not particularly friendly.

Very fast transfer arriving from a intercontinental flight connecting to a European flight. Smooth processing at passing Schengen passport control by using Parafe automated check. Walk from 2E-K to 2F took about 15 mins.

Consistently the worst airport in the world. To make my connection  I had to go through security twice: first they check you as you go to the transfer bus and then it turns out that the only way to enter the Schengen area at 2D is by going through the check-in area. Terrible food - only croissants or super-expensive 'healthy' options.
They insisted weighting my bag  before the security check  and it costed finally 100 euros to check in my bag.

This is such a large airport. I would not like to have a short connection here. I used the Air France lounge in 2F and was severely disappointed. There weren't many food options (nothing hot)  and the coffee machine only half fills the cups. The food was replenished quickly  but as I said  there's a limited choice. In terms of third-party catering  there are a few places but most seem to offer baguettes and salads. After travelling for 14 hours  I wanted more than just a baguette or a slice of quiche.
For international trips (out of EU)  I recommend to be minimun 3 hour before. The airport is huge and theres a lot o people on the lines to passport check and then to the security control.
AF lounges are on the ground floor. Conveniently near the gate  but view and light is because of that location less.
Long queues at security check since domestic flight transfer escalators leads you to airport main entrance. Where you are mixed up with all other travelers departing

The lay-out of this airport is absolutely inefficient. I arrived from Oslo in the left half of Terminal 2F  where we were led to the other half of Terminal 2F to be processed through immigration. Oslo and Paris are both within Schengen  so I was surprised I had to show my passport again. The staff was rude and dismissive. Then I had to walk all the way back to the other half of Terminal 2F again. On the way to Oslo a few days earlier  this was just a matter of clearing a flight of stairs.

Flight from CDG 2F. Access is convenient from Paris center with Roissybus (from Opera) and alternatively by RER B or taxi. Security check is fine but they have added an additional check on cabin luggage weight and size for passengers who checked in online (instead of doing it at the gate)  and since there’s only one row for this check  it creates a bottleneck. Terminal 2F has two piers  one on the left coming out from security check and one on the right (they used to be called F1 and F2). The one on the right has many more restaurants  the one on the left only snack bars and sandwich outlets. The big issue with terminal 2F is that the area near the gates is too small for people to queue and there are not enough seats.

I have been against any transit via airport for years but i must admit that it has really improved a lot. It is much brighter than ever  clear to navigate from each terminal  easy  clean and smell good. There is a lot of shop inside and price are acceptable. Wifi is working well with no time limit. Spotting thru windows is not easy but manageable.
At the night they didn't let us to pass security control and wait in lounge between our connected flights. So we had to sleep on chairs.
Terminal 2G is not really Terminal 2. Terminal 4 should be a correct name as it's a completely separate building.

Terminal 1 at CDG is an absolutely joke. Cramped spaces that can't efficiently cope with the number of passengers they are trying to process on an early Sunday afternoon. Once cleared through security  there is only one place to buy a sandwich. No restaurants or bars etc.

Only few restaurants in 2F Only AF lounge in 2F Had to go to L to find a Priority Pas lounge. The Priority Pas lounge with a poor selection of food. Was not initially not allowed to go back from 2F to L   but was allowed after finding a supervisor. It seems the staff is poorly trainde and arrogant on top!

Quick arrival (no traffic at this time). Fast baggage drop off at terminal 2F (all automated). Modern  big terminal  lots of lighting and windows with great apron views. Fast security and passport control.

Nasty terminal  rude staff  unclean  honestly the nastiest International Airport I've flown I to (T3 that is  the Air France terminal is wonderful) also don't take the train from the airport  they cram you in and held our train for an hour to cram more people into
Not fun to pass through this airport in terminal 2  specially if you have to go to 2E  L or those far away terminals.

No restaurants in the terminal 2E except three small cafes with some fastfood. The Priority Pass lounge was located so far  so you have to take the train and spend approximately 25 min to reach it. Moreover  when you're back  you have to pass the security control again that makes the visit to the lounge almost impossible. Also getting the airport by RER is a kind of challenge  all the announcements are in French  the public transport system is very complicated and lacks navigation much. Even of you find a proper line (B)  there're two different routes on the same line  so you might easily get the wrong train.

Long walk and internal transit train required between arriving at terminal 2E and getting to the L gates which are on a satellite area away from the main terminal. Security is done after passport control and for the L gates the security is done in the satellite area after you’ve been on the transit train  which I did find a bit odd...

Il faut jamais prendre Hop. Leur service était horrible  il y avait pas de climatisation  il faisait hyper chaud. Never fly Hop. Their service was horrible  there was no AC in the plane  it was miserable.

Terminal 2G (HOP !) has unsettling late gate announcements and very limited seating at gate. Two years ago I nearly missed my flight because of a frozen flight display screen  having to leave my beer behind and running to the gate.

Airport is a bit crowded  but clean and walking distances for connecting flights within the Schengen-zone are small. There are some shops and there are many seats inside the terminal. The lounge is basic  but everything you need is there.
This note refers to the terminal G  very poor and without good connexion with other terminals/train/metro
Functional  well organized  good lounge (air France hall K). All went well although there seemed to be a small number of police at the sky priority passport control.
Low cost terminal 3. Bus to the plane  under heavy rain. They took away my bag when I was boarding the plane claiming no to have room for it.

I spent several hours at CDG on a connection from AA to AF. Not a lot of shopping choices aside from food and drink - at least not in Terminal 2F. I was looking since my bags didn't make my flight to CDG on AA.
AVOID going on an Air France/HOP-Mix journey as this means you will have to change planes and go landsline hence security again.

A stupidly complicated airport to navigate. Because of their very poor signalisation for transit passengers  we almost missed our connecting flight even though we had 70 minutes. CDG has constantly proven to be the worst airport in Europe.
My luggage got broken by the staff. Took me 30 minutes to wait there and get a new replacement luggage.

Terminal 2A at CDG in itself is ok for the most part  though as in the rest of CDG the experience is different every time you go there. Parking is fine  but not easy to access (for Terminal 2A)  the staff can be nice (I had a really nice person that time at check-in)  but can sometimes be very rude. Terminal 2A had a lot of expensive stores from various French fashion brands (even though it is more expensive than anywhere else). Food options are ok.
- Very modern and beautiful of the design. - The seats are plenty and ¨look to be¨ good. (I did not sit since I had already had long flight) - Restaurant there are rather expensive.

We only were here to transfer for our flight to Amsterdam  found the airport easy enough to navigate through  although we had done a bit of prior research online so knew where we needed to be going. We only had about 1 hour here so didn't really get to see or do much at the airport

Abysmal airport to use for connecting flights. Ridiculous queues at security even in the Sky Priority line. Convoluted route on a bus to the plane. Overall an awful experience and to be avoided at all costs. To whoever runs CDG take a trip to Schiphol and see how it's done. Avoid at all costs.

My flight was in the Terminal 1 of Paris CDG airport. The oldest part of the airport. Indeed  you can feel that it's not anymore in the present. The check-in was great since there was much people. The security was great too. But the waiting part wasn't great for an international airport. The duty free was very small  lot's of people were waiting for flights and there isn't much place to seat. Moreover  if you have the back luck and you arrive during a boarding process you can barely walk.

Good airport but it depends what terminal you are in. I've been in some terminals that are all luxury and others that are very budget airline looking. Travelling too the airport is easy with RER and the airport itself looks pretty good. Runs fairly smooth and well signed.
One has to spend a lot of time here in order to find to pass a passport control or for connecting flights.

Since i was in transfer from Prague i only have to say that the Lounge at gates K is very good: lot of food but some staff are watching what you are taking and if you take too much food they come to you and say that you have to give the food back and unfriendly staff. Wifi sucks in the airport
Admirals Club was small and didn’t have showers. Also  you cannot get to the Admirals Club upon arrival  only departure

The RER train from the city's core is easy to use and comforatbly delivered us to CDG. There is a bit of an outdoor walk from the train terminal to Terminal 3. Checkin was relatively easy and the security check was quick & efficient. There are a couple of well stocked duty-free stores in the terminal  a Relay news and gift stand with some prepackaged food  and a Pret A Manger for fresher options. The waiting lounge is large and has fun free video game consoles (PacMan or Donkey Kong anyone?) and some PS3 consoles too. Departure form CDG T3 is much better than arrival there  which was awful with no services available.

Even frequent fliers will be confused by the border and security procedures here. The staff will tell you one thing and then moments later scold you for doing what they just told you to do. The EU line at border control doesn't cover all EU countries  in fact it only cover about 4 or 5 so make sure you're in the right line. The staff were rude and didn't seem to know what they were doing either. Thankfully the airline staff are nothing like the airport staff.
T3 is horrible. There are no services on arrival and a long walk outdoors to the connecting train to T1.

Very  very poor food choices in the departure area (just a few very expensive and crowded options). By 8pm already mostly sold out. Getting to the airport very  very painful as usual (RER B broken). I can't think of many major airports with such poor public transport options.

No more stopovers in CDG! Several big planes (full!) come in the morning and only two or three immigration gates opened for so many people. Due to this situation everybody is late for the connecting flight!

Prior to arrival  check the airport map or you will get loose. Since years  it is getting worse and worse. Not organized  no clear signage  border control is a disaster. Choose definitively an other airport for transit. GOOD LUCK :-)

First time in Terminal 1. I liked this Terminal  this one has a lot of history  a lot of charm. Very efficient  fast check-in  fast security check and an amazing view on planes taking off at the boarding hall !
Huge airport  very confusing  shuttle trams between terminals crowded  noisy  unreliable to even run  long lines  not very good air conditioning
Way to much to walk. Signs could be better  some staff don’t speak English in the international concourse.
Terminal 2D is very small with few facilities and too crowd. Our gate was changed just some minutes before the departure time  so we had to run to the other side of the terminal. Not comfortable
People working here are so rude. Fair enough 6 am start is early to be at work...but they got more sleep then I did in economy on a 9 hour flight then I did from KNB

None of the gate numbers make sense. The airport is not laid out logically. It's a maze trying to get from one concourse to another. And the lounge is way too small. Gates are clustered too closely together. Airport operations always run really slow.

As usual  terminal 1 suffers from having to endure the biggest pax loads on arrival as most Asian A380 and others arrive in the morning. The Police is  as usual  over zealous and rude. Sad welcome into France with big queues  every time....

Nothing bad to say of CDG. Great airport. Easy to use (T2F)! From the check-in to the gate not really the fastest terminal but this is quite good. Quick check-in with the "self check-in kiosk" and quick boarding the "self boarding gates".
I enjoy the old style of terminal 1  from a different era  newly renovated. I enjoyed the Star Lounge  quiet and outdoor area was nice.
Really long ways! Be careful when you have a transit flight especially when changing from Terminal 1 to 2 or the other way around. It can take over 1 hour to change the terminals
The terminal G is a misconception - far away  not linked by train  crowded  ugly A complete no go !!!

Very bad airport  long ways to walk  bad signage and information  staff doesnt know a thing  if you are on transit - you will hate this airport. Long queues  sometimes you wait 30minutes then you are told to queue in totally different queue. If your flight is cancelled  no way that you'll find new one. It will take hours to find the transfer info. Try to avoid this airport

Horrible airport  one of the worst  long distances  a lot of unusfull walking. The worst is information  nobody knows anything. You go from point A to B then they send you back to A and so on. It takes a lot of time till you find the right person. There is no decent information about transfer flights in the transfer area  you need to get out of it to get info then you need to get in again. Horrible airport.
Quick and efficient at T1 in the morning  and easier to access than the T2 where the roads were totally jammed... hopefully the CDGVAL saves the situation.

A nice airport. Huge though. If you fly in from Basel you're basically outside at the Terminal which is the farthest away - if you need to get to the main terminal for example you need time. Always plan enough time for this (45 minutes are not enough  my experience)! Staff is friendly. I did not have any time to check out Food and retail service  WiFi and Lounge  hence no rating.
One of the worst Airports in the World. The Transfer to the City Center took me more than 2 hours. The aiport itself is overcrowded.
Très mauvais attitude de la part du personnel de sécurité de CDG. Ces personnes nous hurlent dessus  nous reprocher des choses : les agents manquent cruellement de pédagogie.
all very expensive no good quality. Bad signals. Term F no more passengers capacity  less seats and waiting place
Arrived in Terminal 3. Long que in Immigration line (we were able to be ahead though). Easy walk access to IBIS BDG. Bad access to Le Bus Direct the next morning.
Terminal 2D in CDG is not the best. It feels crowded all the time and doesn't offer much shopping and dining places.

Paris Charles de Gaulle International Airport is a good airport. There are hotels that you can sleep in to rest before your departure or after your arrival. The check-in is a REAL PAIN IN THE BUTT! Trust me! They can make you wait 2 hours until you finally check-in! Same with the security check! AWEFULL! But otherwise their WiFi is fast  the airport has a lot of food and retail services.
Boarding was very badly handled  passengers were shouted at. My boarding card - along with many others - was rejected by the "self-boarding" gates. Massive mess.

World's worst airport to transfer via. Dreadful. Buildings range from architectural delights  to sheds. Bus transfers are massively confusing  drivers erratic and roads appalling to ride on. Avoid 2G. 2E has free lounge seats at far end of terminal building. Security are just plain rude.

Even though I fly in and out of Terminal 1  it's still nice and functional. While the gates can be considered far from check in  the amenities in the main ring after security are plentyful enough. The only downside is limited club space on lower levels. Easy access to city via rail and bus.

Aéroport peu agréable. Les agents sont inexistants. Pour demander des informations  il faut se rendre au bureau d'informations qui ne se trouve que dans certains terminaux de l'aéroport... Personne n'est là pour vous aider.
We waited for 1 hour to move from terminal 2F to 2E L. The trouble was caused by very small number of border polices than the passengers on Saturday.

Interesting - bus transfer massively improved (new lines) Business lounge at Terminal G massively improved The rest of Terminal G is still lamentable We are just waiting for the connecting train to be extended from Terminal E to G

If this unfortunate Schengen Terminal G would be expanded or replaced things at CDG would really work well. This terminal is a true affront to all travellers and much worse than most modern waiting areas in Africa! Crowded  lousy facilities  the AF lounge is simply a joke

The major downturn in terminal 2F is the lack of space in the boarding gates area. Nowhere to sit  hardly acceptable space to stand without having to move every 30 sec to allow another passenger pass ... just awful. Add to this dozens of kids with careless parents and you get the perfect cocktail for the beginning of a memorable trip. Food and drinks are available from various shops but prices are pure robery. Check-in at automate a breeze. Security was fast and courteous. Lounge is also often overcrowded at T2F and food / drinks offer not always renewed.

Changing of planes from Terminal G (Schengen) to international flights has substantially improved over the past year. Separate bus lines for the terminal  with separate waiting queues  shorter driving distance. (It remains an enigma to me why the train moving between Terminal E and M is not being extended to Terminal G - would make it two classes better!

Terminal 3 assez lugubre. Service au sol mauvais (que ce soit pour l'enregistrement  la sécurité ou encore le contrôle des passeports). Pas un sourire ne nous a été adressé. Peu de magasins  salle d'attente tout en longueur... Sensation de hangar.

Vol arrivé à l'heure mais la valise n'a pas suivi. Obligé donc de patienter plusieurs heures à Paris CDG. Sensation d'aéroport désert  les magasins fermaient les uns après les autres dû à l'heure tardive. Personne ne peut vous aider.

Accès depuis le parking voiture facile : navette gratuite qui vous amène directement devant le terminal que vous souhaitez. Aéroport peu spacieux  bonne signalétique. Les comptoirs British Airways se trouvent en sous-sol. Sécurité effectuée par des agents peu motivés et peu souriants. Beaucoup de magasins en zone réglementée.

Contrôle des passeports assez rapide  mais agents peu sympathiques. Un peu d'attente lors de la récupération des bagages. Bonne signalisation pour rejoindre la sortie et la gare SNCF. Mais  peu de personnes pour conseiller et aider.
Terminal 1 is so unique. The only terminal in the prod you can see 2 sign coin to the same direction by 2 different ways. Allow some time to enjoy
At least morning security check goes nicely at an International airport. Public transport connections to CDG from Paris obviously very good.

La zone d'enregistrement est dantesque  un désordre généralisé au Terminal E avec de nombreux postes fermés un jour de grand départ. Le passage de la sécurité est à l'avenant avec deux files ouvertes au satellite M. Les agents doivent bien faire des pauses  nous dit-on.

Very small airport. The aircraft park far from the terminal and you have to cross the tarmac to arrive to the terminal. The passport control queue zone is too small and with just 3 agents and the queue finished outside  on the platform.
Love this small airport where I take many flight for my holiday until 2009...
Bare bones Airport! Well maintained though :) Not many facilities in the terminal  just a bunch of vending machines! The airport staff is helpful.

Typical provincial airport; not a lot happening. No onward transport: no taxis  no buses. However the lady at the information desk was very helpful getting cabs booked. Departing  there is a drink/snack machine and nothing else.
This is a nice airport with unlimited WiFi. It's a simple airport. There has never been too many people so it's simple to travel to London-Stansted  Lyon and La Rochelle.
Positive: -Small airport without a lot of people but with good infrastructure -Car retail services Negative: -Boarding and Deboarding organization
Le restaurant est encore fermé  2 fois visité à cet aéroport (Juin et déc 2021). Pas de caisse au buffet unique (hors zone d'embarquement)
Awful rundown airport with disinterested staff who work at a Glacial pace. Check in was an utter joke as desks did not send problem passengers to one side.

L'aéroprot de Rennes dispose désormais d'un système street view (google map) qui permet de visiter tout l'aéroport  y compris sur le tarmac ! C'est le seul aéroport en France  qui dispose de ce système révolutionnaire.

L'aéroport de rennes est un aéroport très active malgré sa taille   le vfr et l'ifr très active et un embraquement rapide et régulier et les compagnies sont diverses et varié. Les avions peuvent etre tout meme gros   l aéroport peut accueillir des md 11

l'embarquement est très rapide et de qualité. De plus l'aéroport possède 1 bar  un magasin  1 restaurant à ll'étage avec vu sur tout l'ensemble du tarmac  une wi-fi illimité  des toilettes propres  ligne de bus à 50m de l'aéroport (toute les 25min environ).

Nice small airport  with a new terminal. Friendly infrastructures for a very low traffic ! The road to go to the airport is excellent  and there are taxis & bus after each arrival and before each departure.
This airport has a new terminal. Really beautiful and welcoming !!!
Airport has few facilities  and no bus service  and rip-off prices from the few taxis in the town (Rodez Town itself is nice).
